name: Mitch's Fish Market & Sushi Bar
tagline: Fresh-off-the-boat seafood right next to the fishing pier — sashimi doesn't get more local than this.
mustOrder: Sashimi platter — whatever's freshest that day, caught that morning
Garlic shrimp — simple and garlicky, local style
Poke bowl — fresh ahi, rice, and furikake
parking: Parking lot at Pier 38. Easy to find, usually has spots.
talkStory: Run by Mitch Arakawa since the 1990s in an unassuming Kaimuki strip mall on Harding Ave. Mitch hand-selects fish daily from the Honolulu Fish Auction at pier 38, one of the only places in the US where tuna is still auctioned Japanese-style. The sashimi is auction-fresh and priced well below Waikiki omakase spots. Regulars sit at the counter and let Mitch choose — the unofficial omakase. A true neighborhood sushi bar where the chef knows every regular by name.
